summary: Render the Agent Workbench design contract in React and packaged static assets.
rationale: The React Workbench must match the designer assets for the strategy graph and thinking process while staying backed by real BFF/runtime-control projections. Removing ReactFlow and ELK is part of the same UI contract because the strategy graph is now a read-only, fixed-lane timeline instead of an interactive graph canvas.
dependency_rationale: ReactFlow and ELK are removed because the strategy graph is no longer an interactive node canvas. The new implementation is a static BFF-projected lane timeline using local React and CSS only.
verification: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_workbench_static_frontend.py tests/test_react_workbench_cutover_gate.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_agent_workbench_contract.py -q -p no:cacheprovider
CI=1 pnpm --dir apps/web-react exec vitest run src/lib/stream/agentStream.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamReducer.test.ts src/lib/stream/agentStreamView.test.ts --run
deletion_targets: ReactFlow canvas controls, drag, zoom, pan, and selectable graph nodes
CTS as a default Workbench source in fixtures and visible UI
Storybook visual baselines that no longer match the designer assets
stale packaged Workbench static bundle assets
risks: Strategy graph layout must remain readable across mobile and desktop without canvas interactions.
Storybook visual baselines are macOS-specific and must stay aligned with the CI runner.
Packaged static assets must be rebuilt whenever React Workbench output changes.
